topics.title: Canada reaches World Cup knockout stage
topics.summary: Canada and South Africa are preparing for a last-32 World Cup match in Los Angeles, with both countries entering the knockout rounds for the first time.
topics.discussionPrompt: A first trip to the knockout round feels like a big national sports moment. Would you rather watch history being made, or only tune in for the final?

topics.title: Europe heatwave breaks more records
topics.summary: Europe's June heatwave is still disrupting daily life, with the UK breaking another June record and countries including France and Poland adjusting events and wildfire warnings.
topics.discussionPrompt: When extreme weather starts changing public events and travel plans, does it make you more cautious, or do you mostly carry on as planned?

topics.title: 24 Sussex Drive gets design contest
topics.summary: Canada is launching an architecture competition to restore 24 Sussex Drive, the official prime minister's residence, after years of decay and safety concerns.
topics.discussionPrompt: Restoring old public buildings can be symbolic and expensive. Would you preserve a famous old residence, or replace it with something practical and modern?

topics.title: Markets watch jobs and rates
topics.summary: US stocks are heading toward a strong first half of 2026, while investors look ahead to jobs data that could shape interest-rate expectations.
topics.discussionPrompt: Markets seem to react to hints about interest rates before most people notice anything. Do you follow those signals, or just watch everyday prices?

topics.title: Canada wholesale trade likely dipped
topics.summary: Statistics Canada's flash estimate says wholesale trade likely fell 0.7% in May, mainly because of lower sales in machinery, equipment and supplies.
topics.discussionPrompt: Those behind-the-scenes numbers can hint at where the economy is headed. Do you find business statistics useful, or mostly just background noise?
